name: Hozi — Daily CHIRPS Ingest + Engine Run + High-Band Alert

nodes.name: Download CHIRPS Rainfall CSV
nodes.notes: URL is the documented HDX/WFP CHIRPS dataset page for Zimbabwe district rainfall. The direct resource link (CSV) must be confirmed and updated at import time — see automation/README.md. Dataset documented in docs/DATASET-STATEMENT.md: file zwe-rainfall-adm2-full.csv, source HDX/WFP, CC-BY licence.

nodes.name: Write CSV to data/raw
nodes.notes: Writes the downloaded CSV to data/raw/zwe-rainfall-adm2-full.csv under the repo root. HOZI_REPO_ROOT must be set as an environment variable on the n8n VPS instance (e.g. C:\Users\MSI\Documents\CLIENTS CURIOUS INQ\P\POTRAZ\Hozi on Windows, or /opt/hozi on a Linux VPS). On Windows paths use backslashes; on Linux use forward slashes.

nodes.name: Run Engine
nodes.notes: Runs the Hozi Python risk engine from the repo root. Requires Python 3.12+ on PATH of the VPS user running n8n. engine.py reads sample_data/ (prototype) or data/raw/ (live) and writes data/projections.json and app/projections.json. On Windows, use 'python'; on Linux, use 'python3' if needed.

nodes.name: Read Projections JSON
nodes.notes: Reads the engine output from data/projections.json. This is the canonical machine-readable output defined in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md Layer 1. The file contains the 'watch' array with per-district band_sep fields used for threshold alerting.

nodes.name: Send Alert Email
nodes.notes: Demo alert channel: SMTP email. Configure the 'Hozi SMTP' credential in n8n with your mail server details after import. The pilot channel for district officers is WhatsApp Business API (see automation/README.md and docs/DEPLOYMENT-PLAN.md). Replace fromEmail and toEmail with real addresses before live operation. No credentials are stored in this JSON.
